name: crew
description: Autonomous, GitHub-issue-driven dev workflow for Claude Code — plan, build, merge. /crew:pro is the attended planning half: point it at one rough, milestone-sized instruction ticket and it dispatches gatherer → interpreter → planner to survey the code read-only, interview you (every question leading with a code-grounded recommended option), then file granular high-level tickets grouped under an epic parent per feature as native sub-issues, assigned to the resolved milestone (an existing one or a new one you name in the interview), with native blocked_by edges — auto-promoting the work tickets to agent-ready in TODO and closing the instruction ticket (the interview is the sole human touchpoint). A looping orchestrator (/crew:run) then pulls agent-ready issues and drives each through bundled implementation, qa, reviewer, mr-review, ui-review (a measured visual-fidelity gate for UI tickets — it compares the built route's computed typography and font-load fact against the design the design MCP serves, not screenshots), and findings subagents — one MR per ticket, GitHub as the source of truth. /crew:pulls is the autonomous merge half: it runs alongside /crew:run and drains the ready-for-review MR queue by merging them itself with no human green-light, inverting the control surface so merge is the default and the only brake is a human comment — a block/question parks the MR (covering both PR comments and review threads) and the human releases it by resolving the thread or removing the hold label; it is driven by the pull-triage agent (the cross-MR brain that surveys all open MRs and writes a per-run triage tracking issue, opened at run start and closed at run end) and the merge-judge agent (per-MR independent decision + question responder), with conflict/CI fixes dispatched to crew:implementation and main healed in-loop. /crew:adjust onboards a project — scans the structure, validates the test/lint/build/e2e commands, and writes the .crew.rc config file (with a MUST-READ pointer in CLAUDE.md) every component reads at runtime, plus a .mcp.json provisioning the crew MCP servers (Playwright + design). Sets up the required crew GitHub App bot identity (crew-identity) so its comments/commits show as a bot — onboarding stops if the bot cannot be minted. The run loop selects tickets by priority then age.
